channel.title: math.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: A small Banach space $C(K)$ without nice renormings
item.description: arXiv:2606.13294v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We prove that consistently $\omega_1<\mathfrak{c}$ and there exists a compact space $K$ whose Banach space $C(K)$ of continuous real-valued functions is Grothendieck, has density $\omega_1$, and admits no renorming which is strictly convex or sequentially Kadets--Klee.
item.dc:creator: Todor Manev, Damian Sobota, Lyubomyr Zdomskyy

item.title: Planarity of compactifications of $\mathbb{R}$ with arc-like remainder
item.description: arXiv:2409.20455v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We show that if $X$ is an arc-like continuum, then any continuum which is the union of $X$ and a ray $R$ such that $X \cap R = \emptyset$ and $\overline{R} \setminus R \subseteq X$ can be embedded in the plane $\mathbb{R}^2$. Further, we prove that any compactification of a line with remainder $X$ is also embeddable in $\mathbb{R}^2$ -- answering a question of Sam B. Nadler from 1972.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Ammerlaan, Logan C. Hoehn

item.title: Topology and category for singular product spaces
item.description: arXiv:2605.09582v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: For $\kappa$ a regular uncountable cardinal, the higher Baire and Cantor spaces ${}^\kappa\kappa$ and ${}^\kappa2$ (endowed with the ${<}\kappa$-box topology) have been relatively well-studied, but less is known about the case where $\kappa$ is singular. We will consider several spaces of functions and box topologies that could serve as higher Baire and Cantor spaces for singular cardinals. The ultimate focus of the article lies in studying cardinal characteristics of the ideal of $\kappa$-meagre subsets of these spaces.
item.dc:creator: Yusuke Hayashi, Tristan van der Vlugt
